title: Maximum Product of Subsequences With an Alternating Sum Equal to K
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: C++ solution.cpp is both the recommended balanced proposal and the fastest-runtime proposal. No solution-runtime.cpp is shipped because it would be coincident. The minimum-memory champion is implemented in C; see crossReferences.

variants.languageReason: The problem's hot path is branch-light bitset shifting over at most 394 compressed positive-product rows and 3601 alternating-sum bits. C++ gives native execution, inlined std::bitset word operations, and compact contiguous storage, making it the fastest practical and still memory-conscious choice among the allowed languages.
variants.approachJustification: Use product-indexed bitset DP for positive products <= limit, where odd/even length rows are shifted by +x/-x on append. Compress products to the closure generated by input values 1..12, process rows descending to avoid full-table copies, and track zero-product subsequences separately with product-agnostic reachability.

reasoningSummary: The alternating sign depends only on chosen length parity, so positive-product states are (product, parity, sum). The stated k range collapses to sum(nums) <= 1800, and products <= 5000 generated from factors 1..12 collapse to at most 394 rows. Zero-product subsequences need separate reachability because an over-limit positive prefix can become product 0 after a zero.
